2606.31511.title: Falsification, Not Exposure: An Internally Preregistered Placebo-Controlled Decomposition of Self-Repair Feedback in Frozen Small Code Models
2606.31511.paper_summary: In deployment settings where retraining is infeasible, small frozen code models are routinely asked to repair a failed program after seeing their own failing output, usually treated as a retry mechanism. From a Popperian view, a generated program is a conjecture and a test-execution violation is an oracle-relative, executable counterexample, so feedback's value should be attributed not to re-exposure to failing code but to whether the conjecture is opened to external, executable criticism. As the third stage of a falsification-centered measurement program, this study builds a placebo-controlled instrument that decomposes the feedback packet against a blind-resampling baseline at matched output-generation budget and against content-free, shape-matched placebos. The contribution is not a new repair algorithm but a reflexive methodology (packet decomposition, placebo mirroring, matched-budget discordant-pair tests, fresh-generation confirmation, executable audits) that makes both the model's program conjecture and the researcher's "feedback content works" claim falsifiable. Across six HumanEval+/MBPP+ cells with three 0.5B-1.5B frozen models, 290 dead task-cell units (no best-of-8 candidate passing the public tier) were evaluated; the main run produced 7,000 fresh generations and a preregistered follow-up 1,400 more. Blind resampling exceeded bare-code retry by +18 net unlocks (25/7, Holm p=0.0021). Code-plus-facts recovered +18 over bare code (21/3, p=0.00042) and +15 over a generic-bullet placebo (p=0.0041). An instruction-only effect was not distinguishable (+3, p=0.36). Code-plus-facts and blind resampling tied at 26 unlocks each (not equivalence). Six external-controller follow-ups tied a content-free shape placebo. In this regime, falsification helped not as vocabulary or self-critique, but as comparison with external, executable counterexamples.
2606.31511.paper_summary_zh: 在無法重新訓練的部署環境中，小型凍結代碼模型經常被要求在看到自己失敗的輸出後修復失敗的程序，通常被視為重試機制。從波普的角度來看，生成的程序是一個猜想，而測試執行的違規則是一個相對於神諭的可執行反例，因此反饋的價值應該歸因於該猜想是否對外部可執行的批評開放，而不是重新接觸失敗代碼。作為以反駁為中心的測量計劃的第三階段，本研究建立了一個安慰劑對照的工具，該工具在匹配的輸出生成預算下，將反饋包與盲重抽樣基線進行分解，並與無內容、形狀匹配的安慰劑進行比較。其貢獻不是一種新的修復算法，而是一種反思性的方法論（包分解、安慰劑鏡像、匹配預算的不和諧配對測試、新生成確認、可執行審計），使得模型的程序猜想和研究者的“反饋內容有效”主張都可以被反駁。在六個 HumanEval+/MBPP+ 單元中，使用三個 0.5B-1.5B 的凍結模型，評估了 290 個死任務單元（沒有最佳的 8 個候選者通過公共層）；主要運行生成了 7,000 個新生成，並在預註冊的後續中又生成了 1,400 個。盲重抽樣比光代碼重試多解鎖了 18 個（25/7，Holm p=0.0021）。代碼加事實比光代碼多恢復 18 個（21/3，p=0.00042），比一般子彈安慰劑多 15 個（p=0.0041）。僅指令的效果無法區分（+3，p=0.36）。代碼加事實和盲重抽樣各自解鎖了 26 次（不等價）。六個外部控制的後續與無內容的形狀安慰劑持平。在這種情況下，反駁的幫助不在於詞彙或自我批評，而在於與外部可執行反例的比較。
2606.31511.author: Mehmet Iscan et.al.
